# Architecture Decision Records
<!-- Auto-generated by sentinel scan on 2026-06-22; ADR-009 added by the 2026-06-24 delta re-scan (lean inference). Source: verified key decisions (git + file evidence). ADR-008/ADR-009 hand-finalized (full-body inference degraded both scans). -->

## ADR-001: GNU Stow is the sole deployment mechanism

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
Stow provides idempotent, reversible symlinking with package-level granularity (stow/unstow individual tools). The mirrored-path convention is the non-negotiable prerequisite for this to work.

### Decision
GNU Stow is the sole deployment mechanism. Every package's directory tree exactly mirrors the install target so stow can create symlinks without any path translation logic.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting install.sh and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `install.sh`

---

## ADR-002: Bun is the runtime for all TypeScript utility scripts, not Node

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
Bun provides faster startup than Node for one-shot scripts, and eliminating npm deps removes the need for node_modules in the repo. Stated explicitly in streamdeck-build.ts.

### Decision
Bun is the runtime for all TypeScript utility scripts, not Node. No npm packages are used in scripts; only built-in Node/Bun APIs.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting scripts/scripts/streamdeck-build.ts and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `scripts/scripts/streamdeck-build.ts`

---

## ADR-003: Oh-My-Zsh was removed from the shell configuration in favor of manually defined aliases and tool-native integrations (starship, zoxide, direnv, mise, fzf, atuin)

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
OMZ was adding ~200ms to shell startup. All needed functionality (git aliases, web-search) was replicated manually. Comment in .zshrc explicitly records the decision and savings.

### Decision
Oh-My-Zsh was removed from the shell configuration in favor of manually defined aliases and tool-native integrations (starship, zoxide, direnv, mise, fzf, atuin).

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting zsh/.zshrc and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `zsh/.zshrc`

---

## ADR-004: mise replaces all single-language version managers (fnm, pyenv, nvm)

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
Reduces shell init overhead (one eval vs three), unifies per-project tooling declaration in a single file, and removes fnm/pyenv/nvm from the Brewfile.

### Decision
mise replaces all single-language version managers (fnm, pyenv, nvm). A single `mise activate zsh` call handles all runtimes via .mise.toml, .tool-versions, .nvmrc, and .python-version.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting zsh/.zshrc and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `zsh/.zshrc`

---

## ADR-005: BetterDisplay mode-switching writes display parameters directly via CLI rather than loading named 'favorite mode' slots, because the slot-load mechanism is broken in BetterDisplay 4

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
Slot loads were unreliable in the pre-release channel. Direct parameter writes with readback verification are the bulletproof path. Documented as a temporary workaround pending channel revert.

### Decision
BetterDisplay mode-switching writes display parameters directly via CLI rather than loading named 'favorite mode' slots, because the slot-load mechanism is broken in BetterDisplay 4.3.0 pre-release.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh`

---

## ADR-006: bash 3

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
launchd on macOS uses /bin/bash which is frozen at 3.2 due to GPL licensing. Scripts must run without error in that environment.

### Decision
bash 3.2 compatibility is maintained for any script that may be invoked by macOS launchd, using pipe-delimited string tables instead of associative arrays and avoiding bash 4+ syntax.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh`

---

## ADR-007: Per-rig personal configuration is isolated to ~/

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-18

### Context
Keeps machine-specific values (display tag IDs, brightness preferences) out of version control while preserving a single canonical script shared across machines.

### Decision
Per-rig personal configuration is isolated to ~/.config/dotfiles/personal.env (untracked), sourced with an existence guard. All overridable values carry DOTFILES_-prefixed defaults in the script itself.

### Consequences
Adopting this binds the repo to the choice above; reversing it requires revisiting scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh and any dependent scripts/configs.

**Evidence:** `scripts/scripts/bd-apply.sh`

---

## ADR-008: Display config is re-asserted on display-wake (sleepwatcher -W), not just system-wake, via a repo-owned com.lucas.sleepwatcher agent

**Status:** accepted
**Date:** 2026-06-22

### Context
The rig's layout (resolution/rotation/origin) and brightness drift whenever the panels drop off the DDC bus and re-enumerate. The existing wake handler (bd-wake.sh, run via sleepwatcher's `~/.wakeup`) only fired on **system** wake (`-w`). But `pmset -g` shows `sleep 0` — system sleep is held off by caffeinate/backupd/etc. — while `displaysleep` is 10 min and the screen locks at 5 min. So the displays sleep/lock constantly while the system almost never does, and nothing re-applied the config on those events. The Homebrew `sleepwatcher` service plist (regenerated by brew) only wired `-s`/`-w`, and `sleepwatcher` was not even in the Brewfile.

### Decision
Wire sleepwatcher's `-W` (display-wake) hook — alongside `-w` (system-wake) — to the same idempotent `~/.wakeup` → bd-wake.sh, which restores layout (display-restore.sh) + brightness (bd-apply.sh). Make it durable + reproducible via a repo-owned `com.lucas.sleepwatcher.plist.template` (rendered by setup.sh) that supersedes Homebrew's brew-services daemon, plus the missing `sleepwatcher` Brewfile entry. bd-wake.sh also writes a persistent `~/.cache/bd-wake.log` trace.

### Consequences
Display layout + brightness now reapply on every unlock/display-wake, not just the rare system wake. The flag set survives `brew upgrade` (repo agent owns the daemon). Two sleepwatcher instances must not run simultaneously — install must not `brew services start sleepwatcher`. Reversing requires removing the agent template + restoring the brew service.

**Evidence:** `launchagents/Library/LaunchAgents/com.lucas.sleepwatcher.plist.template`, `scripts/scripts/bd-wake.sh`, commit `14b318e`

> **Superseded in part by ADR-009 (2026-06-24):** on Apple Silicon / macOS 26, `-W` registers but its IOKit display-wake notifications never fire — verified from a 2-day `bd-wake.log` gap spanning dozens of display-wake events with zero hook fires. `com.lucas.unlock-watch` replaces `-W` as the load-bearing unlock-recovery trigger; `-w` (system wake) is unaffected.

---

## ADR-009: Replace sleepwatcher -W with com.lucas.unlock-watch (Swift / com.apple.screenIsUnlocked) for post-unlock display recovery on Apple Silicon / macOS 26

**Status:** accepted (supersedes ADR-008 in part)
**Date:** 2026-06-24

### Context
ADR-008 declared sleepwatcher's `-W` (IOKit display-wakeup) hook load-bearing for re-asserting the external monitor's layout and brightness after a lock/unlock cycle. On Apple Silicon / macOS 26, `-W` registers without error but its underlying IOKit display-wrangler notifications never fire; bd-wake.sh is therefore never executed after a lock and the external monitor remains dark. Confirmed 2026-06-24 from a 2-day `~/.cache/bd-wake.log` gap across dozens of "Display is turned on" events with zero hook fires (while `-w` system wakes did fire).

### Decision
Add `com.lucas.unlock-watch`: a swiftc-compiled Swift KeepAlive LaunchAgent (`scripts/scripts/unlock-watch.swift`, built to `~/.local/bin/unlock-watch` by setup.sh's `build_native_helpers()`) that observes the `com.apple.screenIsUnlocked` distributed notification via `DistributedNotificationCenter.default()` and executes `~/.wakeup` on every unlock. launchd has no native trigger for distributed notifications, so a tiny KeepAlive binary bridges the gap. sleepwatcher's `-W` flag is left in place in `com.lucas.sleepwatcher` but reclassified best-effort; sleepwatcher `-w` (true system wake) remains authoritative for system-wake events.

### Consequences
External monitor recovery on lock/unlock is now reliable on Apple Silicon / macOS 26. A Swift toolchain (`swiftc`) becomes a soft install-time dependency — machines without it skip the helper harmlessly (`build_native_helpers()` is `command -v swiftc`-guarded) and `com.lucas.unlock-watch` simply fails to start (no-op). ADR-008's assertion that `-W` is load-bearing is explicitly superseded.

**Evidence:** `scripts/scripts/unlock-watch.swift`, `launchagents/Library/LaunchAgents/com.lucas.unlock-watch.plist.template`, setup.sh `build_native_helpers()`, commit `01fd7a8`; verified live (`/tmp/unlock-watch.log` → `screenIsUnlocked -> ran ~/.wakeup`, fresh `bd-wake.log` fire on unlock).

---
